title: Airtable webhook authorization failure
symptom: All automations triggered by Airtable are failing with a '403: Authorization data is wrong!' error message.
applies_when: Using Airtable to trigger n8n webhook
not_this_if: Webhook node set to 'None' for authentication
root_cause: The error occurs when the authorization data sent by Airtable does not match the expected credentials in the n8n webhook node. This can happen due to changes in header names or values, or if the credentials have become misconfigured or corrupted.
fix_steps: Set the Webhook node’s Authentication to None temporarily.
Trigger the Airtable automation and check if the webhook fires successfully.
If successful, compare the header name and value in the Webhook credentials with what Airtable sends.
Recreate the credentials if discrepancies are found.
agent_instruction: Check the webhook authentication settings and compare with Airtable's headers.
